user_goal: Use the simulator to pick up the red cube and place it in bin_a.
why: Baseline transport instruction should remain stable across all prompt shapes.
Simple greeting instructions should not require any recovery-specific scaffolding.

user_goal: Use the simulator to pick up the red cube and place it in bin_a.
observation: transient motor stall while closing the gripper
why: Without explicit recovery constraints a planner can drift back to pick_and_place because the original task wording dominates.

user_goal: Use the simulator to pick up the red cube and place it in bin_a.
why: Observation recovery should be selected even though the user goal still sounds like transport.

observation: grasp failed but the task is still pending
why: Compact goals depend heavily on prompt/schema constraints because the task text itself is underspecified.

observation: motion interrupted before reaching the wave pose
why: If no matching recovery skill exists, constrained prompts should still permit the task skill instead of forcing a false recovery.
